7335 - {2118=10966, 2119=10966, 2184=<br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br>An incalculable number of creatures have been summoned from memories in the tower. As it turns out, creatures aren't exclusively drawn from memories: the fears, combat expertise and dark desires of warriors have seeped into the tower, until one horrific day when a creature was born: the Sunfreet.<br><br><col=F5B241>Combat</col>: The Sunfreet switches between two states: one in the air and one on the ground. While in the air, you'll want to be careful of its fierce fire-breathing, and it'll take you time to learn the pattern of its attack. While on the ground, it's at your disposal, although it fiercely attacks with its spear-like tail and flaming breath.<br><br>If you should see it inhale, be prepared to turn the other way, unless you wish to be stunned. The Sunfreet will also make a sweeping attack with its tail, for which retreat is the only sensible option. Get it close to death and you'll find it altogether more aggressive. Dragonfire shields won't help protect you.<br><br>You will also have the following handicaps applied: no power-ups, no familiars, no ultimate abilities, no prayers.<br><br><col=F5B241>A character is born</col>: The visual design of the Sunfreet was chosen by attendants of RuneFest 2011, a celebration of all things RuneScape. Based on the concept art and model created at the festival in London, we designed the mechanics to match. Anyone paying close attention might have noticed that the name of the beast is an anagram of 'RuneFest'. Thank you to all the warriors who voted on the design, as it was your 'fears, combat expertise and dark desires' that brought him to life!<br><br><col=F5B241>Quests required to play</col>: None., 2120=10966, 2153=15221, 2121=10966, 2122=60000, 2123=41, 2124=30, 2095=Special: The Fire! 7336 - {2118=10966, 2119=10966, 2184=<br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br><br>An incalculable number of creatures have been summoned from memories in the tower. As it turns out, creatures aren't exclusively drawn from memories: the fears, combat expertise and dark desires of warriors have seeped into the tower, until one horrific day when a creature was born: the Sunfreet.<br><br><col=F5B241>Combat</col>: This version of the Sunfreet is a lot easier than the standard version. The Sunfreet switches between two states: one in the air and one on the ground. While in the air, you'll want to be careful of its fierce fire-breathing, and it'll take you time to learn the pattern of its attack. While on the ground, it's at your disposal, although it fiercely attacks with its spear-like tail and flaming breath.<br><br>If you should see it inhale, be prepared to turn the other way, unless you wish to be stunned. The Sunfreet will also make a sweeping attack with its tail, for which retreat is the only sensible option. Get it close to death and you'll find it altogether more aggressive. Dragonfire shields won't help protect you.<br><br>You will also have the following handicaps applied: no power-ups, no familiars, no ultimate abilities, no prayers.<br><br><col=F5B241>A character is born</col>: The visual design of the Sunfreet was chosen by attendants of RuneFest 2011, a celebration of all things RuneScape. Based on the concept art and model created at the festival in London, we designed the mechanics to match. Anyone paying close attention might have noticed that the name of the beast is an anagram of 'RuneFest'. Thank you to all the warriors who voted on the design, as it was your 'fears, combat expertise and dark desires' that brought him to life!<br><br><col=F5B241>Quests required to play</col>: None., 2120=10966, 2153=15224, 2121=10966, 2122=10000, 2123=41, 2124=30, 2095=Special: The Fire! They will attack with either <col=B4B4BE>ranged</col> or <col=B4B4BE>melee</col>, and have the potential to deal massive damage rapidly.<br><br>Unlike their brethren elsewhere, these airut require <col=B4B4BE>no Slayer knowledge</col> to harm., 4834=21345, 4835=22169}
33571 - {4832=Chargers, 4833=Chargers are vicious, dog-like creatures that are equipped with a spiked helmet. They will charge at their target and begin biting them with <col=B4B4BE>melee</col> attacks., 4834=21343, 4835=26887}
33572 - {4832=Cormes, 4833=Midway through the ambush, Cormes will emerge from the <col=B4B4BE>western</col> entrance of the arena. Cormes can attack from all three sides of the combat triangle. He will attack his target with either <col=B4B4BE>magic</col> or <col=B4B4BE>ranged</col> based on his target's defences.<br><br>Cormes' magic attack can inflict a burn; his ranged attack will drain run energy. Both attacks will hit any targets near his main target.<br><br>Cormes may also charge at a player, stunning them and hitting them for <col=B4B4BE>melee</col> damage., 4834=21389, 4835=26857}
33573 - {4832=Beastmaster Durzag, 4833=After surviving the initial onslaught, Beastmaster Durzag himself will enter the arena from the north. Along with him will be one of his pets, either <col=B4B4BE>Krar</col> or <col=B4B4BE>Tuz</col>.<br><br>The beastmaster will attack with <col=B4B4BE>melee</col> and <col=B4B4BE>ranged</col>. He will also have <col=B4B4BE>bombs, chargers,</col> and <col=B4B4BE>cages</col> at his disposal., 4834=21335, 4835=26885}
33574 - {4832=Cage, 4833=Durzag picks a target and throws a cage at them, preventing them from moving., 4834=21335, 4835=26868}
33575 - {4832=Melee Punch, 4833=The beastmaster punches his target directly, dealing heavy <col=B4B4BE>melee</col> damage., 4834=21335, 4835=26843}
33576 - {4832=Melee Slam, 4833=The beastmaster will slam his main target with a powerful punch. The smash is so powerful, it will deal melee damage to both players and monsters within <col=B4B4BE>2 squares</col> of himself or his target., 4834=21335, 4835=26851}
33577 - {4832=Ranged Attack, 4833=As the beastmaster attacks his main target, he will also launch spines that target those who are not under the attention of one of his pets, dealing ranged damage., 4834=21335, 4835=26848}
33578 - {4832=Enrage, 4833=As the fight progresses, Durzag will grow enraged at his target. As his rage grows stronger, he will deal more damage and reset defensive abilities more often. The effect of his enrage can be countered with the <col=B4B4BE>Freedom</col> ability. Durzag also has a short attention span, and he will forget about his anger after some time if not focused on that target., 4834=21335, 4835=26884}
33579 - {4832=Flurry, 4833=Durzag flails his arms wildly, dragging other targets in front of him and dealing melee damage to anyone caught in the attack., 4834=21335, 4835=26867}
33580 - {4832=Bombs, 4833=Durzag will throw a bomb at a random target, these bombs will follow a player but can be avoided or disarmed. If not defused, it will explode for <col=B4B4BE>magic</col> damage and reset the defensive abilities of anyone caught in the blast. The explosion is powerful, and it will hurt Durzag's beasts just as much as it hurts his targets.<br><br>Once one of his pets has been killed, Durzag will throw a bomb for every target., 4834=21335, 4835=26862}
33581 - {4832=Chargers, 4833=Durzag drops several cages nearby, each containing a trapped <col=B4B4BE>charger</col>. The cage containing the chargers can be opened by a teammate, otherwise they will all open at once on their own at 70%, attacking whoever set them free. If left imprisoned, they will break free themselves, and attack whomever they please.<br><br>When the fight begins, the cages that line the perimeter of the arena will also hold chargers. It is advised to <col=B4B4BE>free them yourself</col> to have full control over whom they can attack., 4834=21343, 4835=26887}
33582 - {4832=Trample, 4833=Anyone standing under the Beastmaster risks getting trampled under his feet. Move away quickly if his initial stomp misses, or else the damage will be unrelenting., 4834=21335, 4835=26842}
33583 - {4832=Pets, 4833=Beastmaster Durzag has two pets that will help him in the fight. Both of Durzag's beasts have some shared attacks, but they still have their own methods of killing their target.<br><br>Durzag's first pet will enter the arena with him. His second pet will arrive when called, after his first beast is around <col=B4B4BE>70%</col> life points., 4834=21337, 4835=26892}
33584 - {4832=Melee attack, 4833=The beast will bite at their target, hitting anyone in the front of them for <col=B4B4BE>melee</col> damage. If their target is up close, then the beasts will focus on biting them.<br><br>The bite can also open a flesh wound, increasing the damage that target takes from attacks. The wound can be partially healed with the <col=B4B4BE>Freedom</col> ability, and it will slowly close itself over time; however, the beast may not let up, and continue to open the wound to prevent it from healing., 4834=21337, 4835=26857}
33585 - {4832=Pounce, 4833=The beast will pounce at a target and begin to attack them with melee. When they pounce, they will also briefly stun the target., 4834=21337, 4835=26861}
33586 - {4832=Krar, 4833=If his target is out of biting distance, Krar will fling spines at them and anyone nearby.<br><br>These spines deal heavy <col=B4B4BE>ranged</col> damage and will also <col=B4B4BE>drain the target's run energy</col>. If his target is out of energy, his attacks will <col=B4B4BE>stun</col> them instead., 4834=21337, 4835=26857}
33587 - {4832=Tuz, 4833=If his target is out of biting distance, Tuz will blast <col=B4B4BE>magic</col> at them and anyone nearby.<br><br>The attack also inflicts a <col=B4B4BE>magical burn</col> that deals further damage regularly.<br><br>The burn can be removed with the <col=B4B4BE>Freedom</col> ability, but Tuz will quickly attempt to apply another., 4834=21336, 4835=26857}
33588 - {4832=Helping hand, 4833=The closer his beasts are to him, the more accurate Durzag's attacks are. Keep his pets at a distance to minimise the damage you take.<br><br>Should the pets die he Durzag will inherit the on-hit ability of his pets (except Cormes)., 4834=21335, 4835=26866}
33589 - {4832=Enough!, 4833=Once the beastmaster has been lowered to <col=B4B4BE>50%</col> of his life points, he will become significantly enraged, increasing the strength and frequency of his attacks.<br><br>If either of his pets are still alive, Durzag will take significantly less damage until they are defeated., 4834=21335, 4835=26884}
33590 - {4832=Gongs, 4833=Gongs are hung at the centre of each of the four walls. The loud ring of the gong will disrupt Durzag's only reliable sense: his hearing.<br><br>Ringing a gong will cost some prayer points, but the result will be effective in confusing Durzag.<br><br>If successful, the gong will completely drain the prayer points of whoever rang it, but the noise will halve the beastmaster's attack speed. The gongs are heavy and fragile, so each one can only be used to discombobulate Durzag once.}
33591 - {4832=Goebie prisoners, 4833=Goebies are cute, rocky creatures that have been held captive by the Beastmaster. Goebies will attempt to assist you in the fight, but they have been left in a weakened state from their imprisonment.<br><br>The evil beasts that held them captive will target the goebies just as much as they target you.<br><br>It is best if they are focused on you, so <col=B4B4BE>provoke</col> any charger or airut that plans to harm one of the little fellows. Remember that freeing the goebies is the goal of this raid; their safety is second to none!, 4834=21367, 4835=26990}
33592 - {4832=Water power, 4833=Yakamaru's attacks are fuelled by the power of water. As he attacks his main target, he will slowly soak them with water, increasing the power of his attacks. Any one close to his main target will also experience the effects of his drench. The only way to counter this is by using <col=B4B4BE>Freedom</col> to shake some of the water off., 4834=21348, 4835=26926}
33593 - {4832=Cowardice, 4833=Players who completely avoid all combat with Yakamaru will enrage him, prompting him to fling projectiles at those he considers cowards in an attempt to lure them into combat.}

33595 - {4832=Ranged Tendrils, 4833=Yakamaru summons small tendrils to pierce anyone outside of his splash distance. These tendrils will always hit his main target and those nearby, but they will not target anyone else preoccupied with another fight., 4834=21350, 4835=26905}
33596 - {4832=Soak, 4833=Yakamaru picks a target and soaks them to the fullest extent, turning them into an entity of pure water. Whoever has this form will gradually take increased damage from it. The effect can be passed between teammates by standing together to avoid damage. After a few passes, the soaked form will dissipate completely., 4834=21348, 4835=26934}
33597 - {4832=Jelly spawn, 4833=Yakamaru spits a <col=B4B4BE>jellyfish</col> out at the team. When it lands, it will create <col=B4B4BE>5 geysers</col> linked to it. Each geyser can be stood on to remove <col=B4B4BE>20%</col> of the jellyfish's life points, but they will also deal <col=B4B4BE>magic</col> damage to anyone on top when they erupt., 4834=21363, 4835=26626}
33598 - {4832=Jellyfish, 4833=Jellyfish are powerful creatures that choose a target and barrage them with attacks. Their attack deals melee damage to anyone in a <col=B4B4BE>3x3</col> area of their main target., 4834=21363, 4835=26630}
33599 - {4832=Central pool, 4833=The fight will begin in the central pool. In this pool, Yakamaru can use his <col=B4B4BE>Soak</col> and <col=B4B4BE>Jelly spawn</col> special attacks., 4834=21348, 4835=26941}
33600 - {4832=Outer pool, 4833=As the fight progresses, Yakamaru will swap pools, giving him new techniques in combat. Yakamaru will choose the first outside pool at <col=B4B4BE>random</col> and will continue from there <col=B4B4BE>clockwise</col> as the fight goes on., 4834=21348, 4835=26941}
33601 - {4832=Southern pool, 4833=In the southernmost pool, Yakamaru has access to much more powerful tendrils. In this pool, he can use his <col=B4B4BE>Soak, Jelly spawn,</col> and <col=B4B4BE>Tendril assault</col> special attacks., 4834=21348, 4835=26941}
33602 - {4832=Tendril assault, 4833=Yakamaru assaults the team with two different tendril attacks simultaneously — <col=B4B4BE>Strangling tendrils</col> and <col=B4B4BE>Whipping tendrils.</col>, 4834=21348}
33603 - {4832=Strangling tendrils, 4833=Strangling tendrils target the players whom Yakamaru considers the most dangerous. These tendrils latch onto the <col=B4B4BE>top five damage dealers</col>, preventing movement and resetting all defensive abilities. They asphyxiate their target, dealing gradually increased damage. The only way to escape is to fight back; you must deal enough damage back at Yakamaru for the choking to stop., 4834=21348, 4835=26927}
33604 - {4832=Whipping tendrils, 4833=Yakamaru goes full assault against his current target, summoning a large tendril growth that whips around wildly. Anyone caught inside this growth will take massive damage rapidly, unless they are being asphyxiated by the <col=B4B4BE>strangling tendrils</col>., 4834=21348, 4835=26912}
33605 - {4832=South-west pool, 4833=In the south-west pool, Yakamaru calls on creatures of the sea to assist in the fight. In this pool, he can use his <col=B4B4BE>Soak, Jelly spawn,</col> and <col=B4B4BE>Sharknado</col> special attacks., 4834=21348, 4835=26941}
33606 - {4832=Sharknado, 4833=Yakamaru begins whirling a cyclone, pulling up dangerous <col=B4B4BE>explosive fish</col>. While the cyclone is still charging, the sharks can be fished out, removing one explosive charge from the attack.<br><br>Any charges remaining will be launched at the team. Yakamaru will attempt to home one fish to each combatant, randomly scattering any spares. When the fish land, they will explode after a few seconds. <col=B4B4BE>Fish</col> them away or just run to avoid taking heavy damage., 4834=21348, 4835=26911}
33607 - {4832=North-west pool, 4833=In the north-west pool, Yakamaru will make use of his environment to attack his targets. In this pool, he can use his <col=B4B4BE>Soak, Jelly spawn,</col> and <col=B4B4BE>Quicksand</col> special attacks., 4834=21348, 4835=26941}
33608 - {4832=Quicksand, 4833=Yakamaru dampens the ground, leaving <col=B4B4BE>two</col> targets standing. Everyone else will be trapped in quicksand, and slowly sink to the bottom. Anyone with mobility will need to <col=B4B4BE>free</col> their teammates. Anyone who sinks below the ground will suffer <col=B4B4BE>instant death</col>., 4834=21348}
33609 - {4832=Northern pool, 4833=In the northernmost pool Yakamaru can use his <col=B4B4BE>Soak</col> and <col=B4B4BE>Jelly spawn</col> special attacks.<br><br>The longer Yakamaru sits in this pool, the harder his body becomes. In his weakest state, Yakamaru will take increased damage, but as his body hardens, he will take less and less damage. To reset his defences, he must be <col=B4B4BE>stunned</col> with an ability. When stunned, Yakamaru will immediately retaliate, damaging every target; the weaker Yakamaru is when he is stunned, the more damage he will deal when he retaliates., 4834=21348, 4835=26941}
33610 - {4832=Poison, 4833=Near the end of each pool, Yakamaru will purge a poison from his body. To properly defeat the pool, someone will need to <col=B4B4BE>absorb the poison</col> and bring it to Yakamaru. If a poisoned player is within <col=B4B4BE>splash distance</col>, Yakamaru will be enticed by the scent of the poison and swallow the player whole when defeated.<br><br>When the poison transfers to Yakamaru, he will return to the central pool, regurgitating the player he swallowed. The regurgitated player will <col=B4B4BE>drown and wash ashore</col>, along with <col=B4B4BE>5 planks</col> that will be needed for later. When Yakamaru returns to the central pool, he will also spawn a <col=B4B4BE>Jelly group</col> to come to his aid., 4834=21348}
33611 - {4832=Resuscitation, 4833=The player who was swallowed and regurgitated by Yakamaru will wash ashore incapacitated. One of their teammates will need to perform CPR before the drowning victim loses breath. If not saved, drowning will cause <col=B4B4BE>instant death.</col>, 4834=21348}
33612 - {4832=Poisoned, 4833=When Yakamaru is still inflicted with the poison he absorbed, he will take increased damage from all attacks., 4834=21357, 4835=26939}
33613 - {4832=Jelly group, 4833=As Yakamaru returns to the central pool, <col=B4B4BE>four jellyfish</col> will spawn and begin moving towards Yakamaru. There is one jellyfish for each side of the combat triangle - <col=B4B4BE>meleefish, magical jellyfish, piercing jellyfish</col> - and one support jellyfish. If any of these jellyfish reach Yakamaru, they will absorb the poison from his body, curing him. If either of the support jellyfish dies, their death will cure Yakamaru., 4834=21363, 4835=26626}
33614 - {4832=Durable jellyfish, 4833=Durable jellyfish can attack with magic, but their main focus will be <col=B4B4BE>healing</col> Yakamaru and other jelly fish., 4834=21361, 4835=26626}
33615 - {4832=Reflecting jellyfish, 4833=While the reflecting jellyfish is alive, the three jellyfish in its group will take decreased damage, with <col=B4B4BE>1/4</col> of that damage being dealt back to players. Reflecting jellyfish are also immune to regular attacks. Each jellyfish under the reflecting jellyfish's protection will damage the reflecting jellyfish for <col=B4B4BE>1/3</col> of its maximum life points when it dies., 4834=21358, 4835=26626}
33616 - {4832=Mirage phase, 4833=Once Yakamaru has been defeated in every pool, he will hide away and create four images of himself, one in each outer pool. These images do not have as many life points as the real Yakamaru, but they are just as powerful offensively. All four images will need to be fought at once; any image that is not in combat will <col=B4B4BE>fling projectiles</col> at the team for increasingly heavy damage., 4834=21352, 4835=26939}
33617 - {4832=Blocking, 4833=Unlike the real Yakamaru, mirage images will not return to the central pool when defeated. Instead, they will leap into the air to regenerate their life points. While this is happening, the team will have <col=B4B4BE>5 seconds</col> to block the pool. If not blocked, the image will respawn with full life points and continue attacking. The <col=B4B4BE>southernmost</col> and <col=B4B4BE>north-west</col> pools require <col=B4B4BE>10 piles of sand</col> from the area to be blocked. The <col=B4B4BE>south-west</col> and <col=B4B4BE>northernmost</col> pools require <col=B4B4BE>10 reclaimed planks</col> to be blocked., 4834=21352}
